description_long: This object is a vintage military-style radio receiver, featuring a robust, boxy metal construction with a dark green finish and visible signs of wear. The front panel is equipped with multiple rotary knobs, toggle switches, and a large central tuning dial, all surrounded by metallic plates and labels. On top, there are several slotted, rectangular modules or battery compartments, each with visible vent holes and metallic latches. The right side of the unit is attached to a secondary box, likely a power supply or speaker, with a circular indented feature and a visible power cord extending from the back. The rear panel displays ventilation grilles, additional ports, and the power cord, emphasizing its utilitarian design. The overall appearance suggests durability and functionality, suitable for use in rugged urban or field environments.
description: A rugged, vintage military-style radio receiver with a green metal body, multiple knobs, switches, and slotted modules on top, attached to a secondary box with a power cord.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main control panel with numerous knobs, switches, a large central dial, and the attached secondary box on the right.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the depth of the main unit, the protruding slotted modules on top, and the side of the secondary box.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals ventilation grilles, ports, the back of the secondary box, and the power cord extending outward.
description_view_3: The right side view displays the side profile of the main unit, the slotted modules, and the attached secondary box, with the power cord visible.
